h1: Senior Legal Resources in Alabama (2026)
introText: Find elder law resources in Alabama. Learn about power of attorney, guardianship, estate planning, and senior financial protections.

poaLaws.description: Alabama recognizes several types of power of attorney. A durable POA remains effective if the principal becomes incapacitated.

guardianship.description: In Alabama, guardianship is established through a court proceeding when an individual can no longer make decisions for themselves.

estatePlanning.description: Estate planning in Alabama involves creating wills, trusts, and advance directives to protect your assets and wishes.
requirements: Valid will must be signed and witnessed
Advance directives should name healthcare agents

elderAbuse.laws: Alabama has laws protecting seniors from physical, emotional, and financial abuse.

reverseMortgage.description: A Home Equity Conversion Mortgage (HECM) allows homeowners 62+ to borrow against home equity.
warnings: Must maintain home and pay taxes/insurance

legalAidOrgs.name: Alabama Legal Aid
legalAidOrgs.services: Free legal help for low-income seniors

legalAidOrgs.name: Eldercare Locator
legalAidOrgs.services: Connects seniors with local resources

faqs.question: How do I set up power of attorney in Alabama?
faqs.answer: In Alabama, you can create a power of attorney by signing a legal document before a notary. It's recommended to work with an elder law attorney to ensure proper execution.

faqs.question: How do I report elder abuse in Alabama?
faqs.answer: Call the Eldercare Locator at 1-800-677-1116 or contact Alabama's Adult Protective Services to report suspected elder abuse.

sources.title: Eldercare Locator
National Academy of Elder Law Attorneys
